_about: Build Nervous System (Stage 4, 'Land dark') client-side feature-flag manifest. Maps a flag name to { enabled, addedInPR, description }. Read once at init time by js/build/build-flags.js into an in-memory object — no localStorage, cookies, network, or server — so it behaves identically in the browser bundle, the Tauri desktop webview, and any future Tauri mobile build. Flag hygiene follows the promote-or-delete rule in docs/build-nervous-system.md: a flag left here for more than 3 merged PRs without being promoted (removed, code kept) or reverted (removed, code deleted) must be flagged in the 4th PR that touches this manifest. `addedInPR` records where each flag's clock started.

pluginPacks.description: Gen 40 domain-pack plugin architecture: assemble the active domain-pack source from self-contained plugin modules under js/packs/ via the pack registry instead of the inline DOMAIN_PACKS map. Behaviour-identical (same runtime pack objects); flip off to fall back to the legacy inline map.

conversationalPackBuilder.description: Gen 42 guided conversational pack builder: replace the blank-text-box pack authoring with a data-grounded question flow (js/agents/question-generator-agent.js), an on-device 'I don't know' resolver (js/agents/uncertainty-resolver-agent.js), a read-only peer-pack index (js/packs/local-pack-index.js), and an incremental pack builder (js/agents/pack-builder-agent.js). Backend (#89) + UI wiring (#91) both merged and tested; flipped on 2026-07-11 for live use in the Validate tab.

conversationalPackBuilderVoice.description: Gen 42 voice input for the conversational pack builder. The free-text answer path is fully implemented and identical to voice (voice is just transcribed text). No permissively-licensed on-device WASM/WebGPU STT model is vendored yet, so the mic is gated behind this flag with a TODO: pick a small Apache/MIT WASM speech model before promotion. When off, buildQuestionView hides the mic entirely and only the typed path shows.

meetingScribe.description: Gen 43 meeting scribe agent (js/agents/meeting-scribe-agent.js): pure, browser-free logic that tags a transcript's segments to whichever chart/query was on screen, flags stakeholder pushback for the existing uncertainty resolver, queues new data requests, and enforces the owner+dueDate+outcome rule before an action item counts as resolved. This flag exists for the promote-or-delete clock even though no UI reads it yet — there is no audio capture, transcription, or Validate/Visualize-tab wiring in this PR (deliberately out of scope, follow-up work). Ships fully dark: no code path is reachable from the app today regardless of this flag's value.
